items.title: Hey all,

items.text:
CASTING CALL FOR FEMALE LEAD.
10-Minute Romance Dramedy Short Film

Not paid, but food + transport reimbursed

Logline: Benny and Veronika have secretly been rekindling their ex-marriage. But when their 6-year-old daughter, Ivy, walks in on them kissing, the pair are forced to explain their complicated relationship - revealing tensions they've tried to ignore.

Please do it once going by script, and it's optional to add an extra take onto the audition where you heavily improvise/adlib the scene, as improv competence, naturalism is something we're looking for with the character.

Character Description: Veronika is a 29-year-old woman, who shares custody of her 6-year-old daughter with a man (Benny) who's clearly still in love with her. Veronika typically makes an effort to be a good mother, she's supportive, loving, and knows when to set boundaries. However, as of late, the quality of her parenting has dwindled as she's become pre-occupied with seeing Benny again. Their chemistry and this strange love affair have her running around giddy as a schoolgirl. But once their daughter walks in on them kissing, Veronika has to seriously consider what she's doing. She realises that getting back together with Benny is not what she wants, despite their connection. Over the short, she struggles to commit to letting him go, as she's still attracted to him, and she doesn't want to hurt Benny or her daughter. This leads to her sending mixed signals which others might see as manipulative, emotional, or unreadable - but in reality it's just her being human.

Scene Context: A few days before, their 6-year-old daughter walked in on them kissing and the confusion of it all led Veronika to kicking Benny out of her house that night. This is the first time the couple has spoken to each other since. Veronika has invited Benny over to fix the broken sink. She goes into this conversation wanting to firmly communicate that they should be apart, but her insecurities around their relationship bubble to the surface - and she also gets a subtle thrill from confrontations with her lover. This isn't your conventional argument. No one raises their voice. There are often long pauses between responses. Benny is floundering in his head trying to find the right answers. Veronika is calculated at first but gradually breaks down.

Email for script to audition!

Will be filming in april.

CASTING CALL FOR FEMALE LEAD.
10-Minute Romance Dramedy Short Film

Not paid, but food + transport reimbursed

Logline: Benny and Veronika have secretly been rekindling their ex-marriage. But when their 6-year-old daughter, Ivy, walks in on them kissing, the pair are forced to explain their complicated relationship - revealing tensions they've tried to ignore.

Please do it once going by script, and it's optional to add an extra take onto the audition where you heavily improvise/adlib the scene, as improv competence, naturalism is something we're looking for with the character.

Character Description: Veronika is a 29-year-old woman, who shares custody of her 6-year-old daughter with a man (Benny) who's clearly still in love with her. Veronika typically makes an effort to be a good mother, she's supportive, loving, and knows when to set boundaries. However, as of late, the quality of her parenting has dwindled as she's become pre-occupied with seeing Benny again. Their chemistry and this strange love affair have her running around giddy as a schoolgirl. But once their daughter walks in on them kissing, Veronika has to seriously consider what she's doing. She realises that getting back together with Benny is not what she wants, despite their connection. Over the short, she struggles to commit to letting him go, as she's still attracted to him, and she doesn't want to hurt Benny or her daughter. This leads to her sending mixed signals which others might see as manipulative, emotional, or unreadable - but in reality it's just her being human.

Scene Context: A few days before, their 6-year-old daughter walked in on them kissing and the confusion of it all led Veronika to kicking Benny out of her house that night. This is the first time the couple has spoken to each other since. Veronika has invited Benny over to fix the broken sink. She goes into this conversation wanting to firmly communicate that they should be apart, but her insecurities around their relationship bubble to the surface - and she also gets a subtle thrill from confrontations with her lover. This isn't your conventional argument. No one raises their voice. There are often long pauses between responses. Benny is floundering in his head trying to find the right answers. Veronika is calculated at first but gradually breaks down.

Email for script to audition!

Will be filming in april.

items.text:
📣🎬 CASTING CALL – SHORT MOCKUMENTARY FILM

We are casting speaking roles and male athlete extras for RAPID, a 10-minute mockumentary graduation film produced as part of the BA Filmmaking course at UWE Bristol.

The film will be shot in Bristol from 2nd–6th March 2026. This is a student project, unpaid with travel expenses and food provided, intended for university assessment, portfolio use, and festival submission.

STORY:

RAPID follows a small documentary crew as they capture former sprint star Ian Hitchworth’s attempted comeback through a chaotic charity race. 

Told in a deadpan mockumentary style, the film blends awkward comedy with moments of vulnerability. 

Humour comes through stillness, micro-expressions, and uncomfortable silences — very performance-led and character-driven.

SPEAKING ROLES:

SHOP CUSTOMER
Female, 60+
Small speaking role confronting Ian in the sports shop where he works. Dry, natural delivery.

SAM – CHANGING ROOM ATHLETE
Male, 20s
Sarcastic and cynical. Quietly undermines Ian with dry interrogation and subtle looks to camera. Comedy comes through restraint.

DUNCAN – CHANGING ROOM ATHLETE
Male, 20s
Slow-witted but fascinated by the camera. Emotionless, slightly odd presence. Competing in the trial with no real passion — just wants something to do.

EXTRAS:

MALE ATHLETE EXTRAS – CHANGING ROOM & RACE SCENES
Male, 18–35
Looking for male extras to play track athletes in changing room and race scenes.

Comfortable in sportswear and performing in a natural mockumentary-style environment. Strong reactions and subtle on-camera presence important.

Great opportunity to gain on-set experience, receive a screen credit, and obtain showreel material.

KEY INFO:
📍 Location: Bristol
📅 Shoot dates: 2nd–6th March 2026 (likely one day only for most roles)
💼 Payment: Voluntary (travel expenses & food provided)

TO APPLY:
Please send a headshot and CV (or Spotlight / Mandy link) to:
📩 rapidfilm2026@gmail.com

items.title: 📣🎬 CASTING CALL – SHORT MOCKUMENTARY FILM

items.text:
Casting call!!!
Passion project, (unpaid) but percentage of any profits will be agreed.

Festival submissions

Filmed in Lytham, Lancashire. 

Logline

A troubled man returns to the seaside town where a tragedy destroyed more than just his life, confronting a figure from his past in a haunting search for forgiveness and closure and the biggest question, is Redemption always attainable, or is something you have to live without. 

Just One More is a gritty, character-driven short film set on the Lytham seafront. The story follows Shawn, a man burdened by guilt and regret, as he meets Rob, someone from his past connected to a tragic event that changed both their lives. Through intense dialogue and fragmented memories, the film explores themes of addiction, blame, grief, and redemption, culminating in a powerful psychological twist.

The film has a gritty, realistic tone with strong emotional performances and a grounded British setting. We are looking for actors who can deliver subtle, naturalistic performances and convey deep internal conflict.

 

Rachel 

Age Range: mid 20s to mid-30s

Gender: Female

Ethnicity: Open

Description:

A silent or minimal-dialogue role representing family and continuity. She appears  with a pram and young child, triggering Rob’s emotional breakdown and symbolising what he lost. She then has a devastating emotional breakdown, will include an action scene as she attacks the lead protagonist.

Performance Notes:

This role relies on subtle presence, naturalism and raw emotion. No heavy dialogue required.

MELISSA

Age Range: Mid–20s to early 30s

Gender: Female

Ethnicity: Open

Accent: Northern / British (flexible depending on casting)

Description:

Melissa is Shawn’s partner and represents the life he could have had. She is warm, playful, and emotionally open, with a natural charm and energy. In the present day, she appears briefly but symbolically, embodying love, hope, and what was lost. In flashbacks, she is caring but frustrated by Shawn’s drinking and reckless behaviour.

I'm aiming to get this short film shot within the next two weeks. It is only approximately 5 minutes long and requires minimal dialogue.

Hey Everyone,

Currently planning a student film around the 16th - 22nd March (more dates planned in early April but TBC)

This is a story about the fear of growing up too quick and how it's not a bad thing to have that child-like wonder in everything.

Contact: adamwilliamson2803@gmail.com if you are interested!

items.text:
CASTING CALL for MASZED
Final Year Film, Edinburgh Napier University

Filming: 27–28 February & 3 March 2026
Location: Edinburgh
Unpaid student production. Travel and food provided.

STORY
Polish immigrant Tomasz spent his teenage years in Edinburgh where he experienced bullying and racial abuse.
20 years later, he ends up working with Shelley, one of his former abusers.

WE CURRENTLY ESPECIALLY NEED:
Teen Bullies
Featured roles
Must be 18+
Playing age 16–17
Scottish
Shoot dates 27 and 28 February

OTHER ROLES

Secretary
Small speaking role
Any age, gender, ethnicity
Shoot date 1 March

Supporting artists - extras (school)
Must be 18+
Playing age 16–17
Required 28 February

Party Supporting Artists - Extras, Halloween party
Any gender
Shoot date 3 March

All filming takes place in Edinburgh.

Contact
[40721281@live.napier.ac.uk](mailto:40721281@live.napier.ac.uk)

More info:

[https://www.instagram.com/maszedfilm/](https://www.instagram.com/maszedfilm/)
[https://www.gofundme.com/f/maszed-a-film-about-bullying-racism-and-wounds-that-last](https://www.gofundme.com/f/maszed-a-film-about-bullying-racism-and-wounds-that-last)
